title: Add pre-checkpoint and restore with previous
body:
Add --pre-checkpoint option to checkpoint and --with-previous option to restore, for less dump-time when migrating

Now it only works with two-step checkpoint, the first is pre-checkpoint , the second is checkpoint with previous. It can be used like:

1. podman container checkpoint -P -e pre-checkpoint.tar.gz -l
2. scp pre-export.tar.gz destination
3. podman container checkpoint --with-previous -e checkpoint.tar.gz -l
4. scp export.tar.gz destination
5. ssh destination
6. podman container restore --import-previous=pre-checkpoint.tar.gz --import=checkpoint.tar.gz

Signed-off-by: Zhuohan Chen <chen_zhuohan@163.com>

comments.body: Something fail but I don't know why it happened, could someone help me?
What if I want to pre-dump several times? Does it support that?

> What if I want to pre-dump several times? Does it support that?

Not yet. Now it only supports two-step checkpoint. It may be supported later.

CRIU's pre-dump support does not work on aarch64, because aarch64 has no kernel support for dirty page tracking. All other architectures CRIU supports (pp64le, s390x, x86_64) can do dirty page tracking. This is something Podman could already test for to make sure pre-copy is not used on aarch64. Maybe also something for the documentation.

The whole pre-dump support has no test yet, can you include a test to the existing checkpoint tests in e2e?

> CRIU's pre-dump support does not work on aarch64, because aarch64 has no kernel support for dirty page tracking. All other architectures CRIU supports (pp64le, s390x, x86_64) can do dirty page tracking. This is something Podman could already test for to make sure pre-copy is not used on aarch64. Yes, I have tried to add two test, about export and not export with pre-checkpoint. Except to get your review.
